questions.question: Which flex pattern makes a footer stick to the bottom?
questions.explanation: Use `flex-direction: column` on body, `flex: 1` on main content to push footer down.

questions.question: Which pattern creates equal-height cards with button at bottom?
options: Card as column flex container, button: margin-top: auto
questions.explanation: Make each card `display: flex; flex-direction: column` with `margin-top: auto` on the button.

questions.question: How do you create responsive cards without media queries?
questions.explanation: `flex-wrap: wrap` with `flex: 1 1 250px` creates responsive cards that wrap automatically.

questions.question: Which pattern is best for a nav bar with logo and links?
questions.explanation: Flexbox with `justify-content: space-between` is ideal for nav bars.

questions.question: What layout is NOT ideal for Flexbox?
questions.explanation: Flexbox is 1D. Complex 2D layouts are better suited to CSS Grid.

questions.question: How do you create a Holy Grail layout with Flexbox?
options: Column direction on body, then row on main for sidebar + content
questions.explanation: The Holy Grail layout uses flex-direction: column on the body and display: flex on the main area for sidebars.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `margin-left: auto` on a flex item?
options: Pushes that item to the right, separating it from previous items
Adds space to the left of the item
questions.explanation: Auto margins in flexbox absorb available space, pushing the item to the far end of the axis.

questions.question: Which pattern is best for a centered login form?
options: Body as flex container with justify-content: center and align-items: center
questions.explanation: Flexbox centering (align-items + justify-content both set to center) is the simplest way to center a form.

questions.question: How would you create a three-column layout where the center column is wider?
options: flex: 1 on side columns, flex: 2 on center column
questions.explanation: Using different flex-grow values creates proportional columns. flex: 2 on center makes it twice as wide.

questions.question: What pattern creates a media object (image left, content right)?
options: display: flex on container, fixed width on image, flex: 1 on content
questions.explanation: The classic media object uses flexbox — image gets a fixed width, content fills remaining space with flex: 1.

questions.question: How do you create equal-width columns with Flexbox?
questions.explanation: flex: 1 on each item sets flex-basis: 0 and flex-grow: 1, making all items equal width.

questions.question: What is a common pattern for a dashboard widget grid?
options: flex-wrap: wrap with flex: 1 1 300px on widgets
questions.explanation: Wrapping flex items with a flex-basis (300px) creates a responsive dashboard grid.

questions.question: How would you create a vertically centered hero section?
options: display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center on hero
questions.explanation: Flexbox centering on both axes is the most reliable way to center content in a hero section.

questions.question: What flex pattern creates a navbar where nav links are on the right?
questions.explanation: space-between pushes logo left and nav links right, then you can use margin-left: auto on the nav group.

questions.question: How would you build a card layout where cards have equal height?
options: align-items: stretch (default) on the flex container
questions.explanation: By default (stretch), all flex items in a row have equal height — this is a key advantage of Flexbox for cards.

questions.question: What is the margin-trick for pushing a flex item to the end?
options: margin-top: auto (in column) or margin-left: auto (in row)
questions.explanation: Auto margins on the cross or main axis push items to the end, useful for footer buttons or nav links.

questions.question: Which flex pattern helps create an input group with a button attached?
options: display: flex on container, flex: 1 on input, fixed width on button
questions.explanation: Flexbox makes input groups easy — the input gets flex: 1 to fill space, and the button stays at its natural width.

questions.question: How do you build a centered pagination component with Flexbox?
options: display: flex; justify-content: center on the pagination container
questions.explanation: justify-content: center on a flex container perfectly centers pagination links as a group.

questions.question: What is the best approach for a sticky sidebar with Flexbox?
options: Use flex: 0 0 300px on sidebar and flex: 1 on main content
questions.explanation: A fixed-width sidebar (flex: 0 0 300px) paired with flexible main content (flex: 1) creates a classic sidebar layout.

questions.question: How would you create a centered badge or pill element?
options: display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center
questions.explanation: inline-flex with centering on both axes, plus padding and border-radius, creates perfectly centered badges.
